codeReview.summary: The import order fix in main.go correctly follows the CLAUDE.md pattern. The change reorganizes imports from a fragmented structure to the proper three-tier grouping: standard library imports first (context, embed, fmt, net/http, net/url, os, strings, time), followed by third-party libraries with a blank line separator (logrus, gotgbot/v2, gotgbot/v2/ext), then internal project packages with a blank line separator (all alita/* imports). The fix is minimal (9 lines changed), focused solely on import organization, and introduces no functional code changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CLAUDE.md import pattern documentation is clear and the worker followed it correctly. The pattern is: stdlib → blank line → third-party → blank line → internal packages. This is explicitly documented in the Code Style Guidelines section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CLAUDE.md lines 52-61 shows the expected import pattern with blank line separators between groups. The fix applied this pattern exactly: main.go now has stdlib imports, blank line, logrus + gotgbot imports, blank line, then all internal alita/* imports.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The bugfix-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. The worker verified the fix with gofmt (no output = properly formatted), golangci-lint (0 issues), and go build (successful compilation).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows verification.commandsRun with exitCode 0 for all three validators: gofmt -l main.go, golangci-lint run main.go, and go build -o /tmp/alita_test main.go

summary: Review passed. The fix-import-order feature correctly reorganizes main.go imports to follow CLAUDE.md pattern with stdlib → third-party → internal grouping and blank line separators. The change is minimal (9 lines), focused only on import ordering, and all validators pass (gofmt, golangci-lint, go build). No functional code changes were made. Tests continue to pass.
